name: Bond type in NaCl
question_text: Sodium chloride (NaCl) is held together by which primary bond type?
general_feedback: Sodium (a metal) transfers its outer electron to chlorine (a non-metal), producing Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions. Electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions is an ionic bond.
correct_feedback: Correct — Electron transfer produces Na⁺ and Cl⁻; the electrostatic attraction is an ionic bond.

answers.feedback: Electron transfer produces Na⁺ and Cl⁻; the electrostatic attraction is an ionic bond.
Covalent bonds share electron pairs between atoms; Na and Cl instead transfer an electron.
Metallic bonding occurs between metal atoms sharing delocalised electrons — NaCl contains a non-metal.
Hydrogen bonds are weak intermolecular forces involving H bonded to N, O, or F — not present in NaCl.
